Perfect. I've successfully read all five iteration documents. Let me extract and organize the comprehensive findings:

---

## COMPREHENSIVE EXTRACTION: ALL ITERATIONS (1-5)

### ITERATION 1

**User Study Findings:**
- Participant struggled with selecting and moving notes; system highlighted note stems instead of noteheads
- Cursor failed to transform into a "hand" icon for movable elements
- Complex rhythmic adjustments (combining 8th and 16th notes) produced misaligned notation
- Excessive cognitive load from unlabeled toolbar buttons

**Feature/UI Changes Requested:**
1. Cursor and Selection Accuracy: Implement proper hover states and "hand" cursor for movable elements; fix note selection to target noteheads not stems
2. Rhythmic Editing: Support complex rhythmic combinations with proper alignment
3. Toolbar Clarity: Add labels to buttons to reduce cognitive load
4. Theory Inspector Verbosity: Constrain LLM output to concise, bulleted feedback instead of excessive prose
5. Theory Inspector Contextual Alignment: Ensure AI suggestions align with user's stated musical goals (e.g., maintaining C Major tonality)
6. Empty State Scaffolding: Add clickable, contextual starter prompts in the Theory Inspector chat (e.g., "Check my voice leading," "Suggest a passing chord here")
7. Targeted Editing: Enable users to highlight specific measures and request AI-applied changes with user-specified constraints

**Generation Engine Fixes:**
1. Rhythmic Monotony: Engine must match rhythmic integrity of input melody (not default to half notes)
2. Voice Independence: Prevent soprano and alto from unison throughout; ensure voice-leading best practices
3. Clef Assignment: Correct tenor clef assignment (was using mezzo-soprano)

**Prioritization Hints:** Clef errors explicitly noted as damaging to user trust; voice leading and rhythmic accuracy deemed foundational to system credibility.

---

### ITERATION 2

**User Study Findings:**
- System fails to recognize pickup measures (anacrusis), causing premature accompaniment and misaligned harmonic rhythm
- User parameters (key, mode) drop when navigating between screens
- Participant unfamiliar with "SATB" terminology
- Deleting a note left measures visually incomplete or altered surrounding note durations
- Theory Inspector integration to score described as "very clunky" and occasionally acted as destructive undo
- MIDI playback crashed repeatedly and played at inaudible volume
- Theory Inspector interface had basic scrolling bugs, required full-screen browser view

**Feature/UI Changes Requested:**
1. Anacrusis/Pickup Measure Support: Add pre-generation setting to explicitly define pickup measures; allow global accompaniment offset
2. State Persistence: Retain user parameters (key, mode, settings) across all navigation; prevent re-entry on screen transitions
3. Plain-Language Accessibility: Replace "SATB" and domain-specific terms with layman-accessible tooltips
4. Rest Placeholders: When notes are deleted, fill measures with rests to maintain visual completeness
5. Theory Inspector Integration: Refine front-end to make applying AI suggestions a precise edit (not destructive undo)
6. Rhythmic Complexity Control: Add parameter to choose between "whole notes" (static) and "running notes" (active) before generation
7. Theory Inspector Simplification: Default to simplified, actionable suggestions; allow optional expansion to see underlying rule-based decision tree
8. Theory Inspector Responsive Design: Fix scrolling bugs; ensure overlay doesn't obstruct primary workspace
9. MIDI Playback Stability: Fix crash issues and inaudible volume problem

**Prioritization Hints:** MIDI playback stability and Theory Inspector responsiveness noted as "paramount"; state persistence flagged as critical for workflow continuity.

---

### ITERATION 3

**User Study Findings:**
- System successfully preserves melody and offers robust customization (outperforms probabilistic model)
- Theory Inspector analyzes chords in isolation rather than understanding transitional context (e.g., missed ii-V-I cadences, tritone substitutions)
- User attempted to change genre to "jazz" and key to "minor"; system failed to execute transformations correctly
- Participant explicitly requested localized "highlight-to-generate" workflow for specific measures

**Feature/UI Changes Requested:**
1. Context-Aware Chord Analysis: Overhaul Theory Inspector LLM prompt to evaluate harmonic progressions sequentially (identify cadences, substitutions, voice-leading context) rather than analyzing chords in isolation
2. Localized "Highlight-to-Generate" Functionality: Transition from "generate-all" model to allow users to select specific bars/notes and request alternate harmonic voicings for that section only
3. Expressive Sovereignty Boundary: Ensure generative logic NEVER automatically inserts expressive/dynamic markings (phrasing, dynamics, staccatos, legatos); reserve these for human interpretation only
4. UI Design: Actively prompt users to overlay their own articulations after harmonic generation

**Generation Engine Fixes:**
1. Parameter Execution Accuracy: Audit constraint-solving engine to ensure genre (jazz) and mode (minor) parameters rigidly enforce correct harmonic rulesets without corrupting melody integrity

**Prioritization Hints:** "Bare bones framework" (chords only) approach emphasized as philosophical boundary; localized editing identified as enabler of co-creative partnership. Note: localized bar regenerate was briefly prototyped then removed; re-scope against classical-only baseline.

---

### ITERATION 4

**User Study Findings:**
- User immediately confused by configuration settings: "portal, mixed and flowing" and "backing chords" terminology unclear
- Participant became trapped in generation screen, unaware of navigation options ("How do you go back to...")
- User didn't know they could manipulate the score (click and drag notes) until moderator explicitly instructed
- UI layout issues: participant couldn't scroll with Theory Inspector open
- Interaction glitch: clicking a note failed to trigger action; unclear whether rendering failed or interaction failed
- Participant exhibited UI discoverability problems

**Feature/UI Changes Requested:**
1. Clarify Generative Terminology: Replace jargon ("portal, mixed and flowing," "backing chords") with tooltips or layman-accessible descriptions of actual musical outcomes
2. Enhance Navigational Discoverability: Rename "document tab" to something actionable like "Edit Configuration" or "Settings"; reduce navigational friction
3. Improve Interactive Affordances: Implement cursor changes, hover states, or brief interactive onboarding overlay to communicate that user can click/drag notes to edit
4. Resolve Layout and Scrolling Conflicts: Refine responsive design so pedagogical overlays don't obstruct primary workspace
5. Optimize System Responsiveness: Ensure immediate, tactile feedback for every interaction (clicking notes, etc.)

**Prioritization Hints:** Immediate, tactile feedback critical for maintaining user trust; visual affordances needed at entry point (new users).

---

### ITERATION 5

**User Study Findings:**
- User exhibited cognitive overload and usability barriers despite appreciating interactive nature
- Participant stated "My music theory is really rough" — struggled to understand Theory Inspector explanations
- User showed signs of visual overload: "Okay, I clicked on so many things now. I don't know what I'm looking at"
- Participant unfamiliar with instrument names; hesitated during setup ("I don't actually know what all the instruments are")
- Expressed physical interaction friction: "I wish I had a mouse"; confused by "Click or drag" instructions
- Trackpad-specific usability issues noted

**Feature/UI Changes Requested:**
1. Plain-Language Theory Translations: Translate academic constraints in Theory Inspector into accessible language for non-theorist users/jammers
2. Progressive Disclosure and Reset: Hide advanced parameters until needed; provide clear "reset to default" state so users don't become trapped in unrecognizable workspace
3. Clarify Instrument Selection: Add auditory previews, instrument family groupings, or visual icons to reduce friction during setup
4. Trackpad Optimization: Ensure sandbox is optimized for trackpad users; implement alternative interaction methods (keyboard shortcuts, tap-to-move) for users without mouse

**Prioritization Hints:** Accessibility (theory level, physical interaction, progressive disclosure) flagged as foundational for user confidence; "Glass Box" pedagogical experience blocked by cognitive overload.

---

## ZERO-AMBIGUITY IMPLEMENTATION SUMMARY

**Critical Path (Blocking Core Usability):**
- MIDI playback stability (crashes, inaudible)
- State persistence across navigation
- Cursor/hover affordances for interactive elements
- Plain-language terminology (remove "SATB," "portal," "backing chords")

**High Priority (Enabling Core Features):**
- Anacrusis/pickup measure support
- Clef assignment correctness
- Voice independence (prevent unison)
- Rhythmic accuracy matching input melody
- Theory Inspector simplification (bulleted, concise, optional expansion)
- Theory Inspector contextual prompt starters
- Rest placeholder on note deletion

**Medium Priority (Workflow Enhancement):**
- Localized "highlight-to-regenerate" (re-scope against classical-only baseline)
- Rhythmic complexity parameter (static vs. running notes)
- Targeted AI editing with measure selection
- Interactive onboarding for click/drag editing
- Responsive design (overlay scrolling bugs)
- Trackpad interaction alternatives

**Lower Priority (Polish/Accessibility):**
- Instrument family groupings and auditory previews
- Progressive disclosure of advanced parameters
- Theory Inspector visual hierarchy refinement

```text
50	  const { genre, violationType, violationContext, scoreContext } = body;
51	
52	  if (!genre || !scoreContext || scoreContext.length === 0) {
53	    return NextResponse.json(
54	      { error: "Missing required fields: genre, scoreContext" },
55	      { status: 400 },
56	    );
57	  }
58	
59	  const { apiKey, model } = getServerOpenAIEnv();
60	
61	  if (!apiKey) {
62	    return NextResponse.json(
63	      { error: "No API key configured. Structured suggestions require an active LLM connection." },
64	      { status: 503 },
65	    );
66	  }
67	
68	  const explanationLevel = resolveExplanationLevel(body.explanationLevel);
69	
70	  const suggestionExplanationMode: SuggestionExplanationMode =
71	    isSuggestionExplanationMode(body.suggestionExplanationMode)
72	      ? body.suggestionExplanationMode
73	      : "full";
74	
75	  const taxonomySection = getTaxonomyContext(genre, violationType);
76	
77	  const systemPrompt = buildStylistStructuredPrompt({
78	    genre,
79	    taxonomySection,
80	    violationType,
81	    violationContext,
82	    scoreContext,
83	    explanationLevel,
84	    suggestionExplanationMode,
85	  });
86	
87	  const messages = [
88	    {
89	      role: "user" as const,
90	      content: body.userMessage ?? "Suggest corrections to resolve the detected violations.",
91	    },
92	  ];
93	
94	  try {
95	    const result: SuggestResponse = await structuredCompletion(
96	      apiKey,
97	      model,
98	      systemPrompt,
99	      messages,
100	      suggestResponseSchema,
101	      "suggest_corrections",
102	    );
103	
104	    // Filter out corrections with invalid "note_X" indices
105	    const validCorrections = result.corrections.filter((c) => {
106	      const match = c.noteId.match(/^note_(\d+)$/);
107	      if (!match) return false;
108	      const idx = parseInt(match[1], 10);
109	      return idx >= 0 && idx < scoreContext.length;
110	    });
111	
112	    const stripped =
113	      suggestionExplanationMode === "minimal"
114	        ? {
115	            corrections: validCorrections.map((c) => ({
116	              ...c,
117	              rationale: "",
118	            })),
119	            summary: "",
120	          }
121	        : { corrections: validCorrections, summary: result.summary };
122	
123	    return NextResponse.json(stripped);
124	  } catch (err) {
125	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
126	    return NextResponse.json(
127	      { error: `Suggestion request failed: ${message}` },
128	      { status: 502 },
129	    );
130	  }
131	}
132
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * Parse and route `<<<INTENT>>>` JSON blocks emitted by the Theory Inspector.
3	 *
4	 * The LLM prompts already instruct the tutor to emit a JSON object describing
5	 * actions the user asked for that the chat cannot perform directly (mood
6	 * changes, pickup edits, regenerate, etc.). We parse that block client-side
7	 * and surface a confirmation bubble — accepting it writes to the generation
8	 * config store and/or navigates to the right screen.
9	 */
10	
11	import { z } from "zod";
12	
13	export const INTENT_MARKER = "<<<INTENT>>>";
14	
15	const baseIntent = z.object({
16	  reason: z.string().max(400).optional(),
17	});
18	
19	const setMoodIntent = baseIntent.extend({
20	  action: z.literal("set_mood"),
21	  value: z.enum(["major", "minor"]),
22	});
23	
24	const setGenreIntent = baseIntent.extend({
25	  action: z.literal("set_genre"),
26	  value: z.enum(["classical", "jazz", "pop"]),
27	});
28	
29	const setRhythmDensityIntent = baseIntent.extend({
30	  action: z.literal("set_rhythm_density"),
31	  value: z.enum(["chordal", "mixed", "flowing"]),
32	});
33	
34	const setPickupBeatsIntent = baseIntent.extend({
35	  action: z.literal("set_pickup_beats"),
36	  value: z.number().finite().min(0).max(8),
37	});
38	
39	const regenerateIntent = baseIntent.extend({
40	  action: z.literal("regenerate"),
41	});
42	
43	const openDocumentIntent = baseIntent.extend({
44	  action: z.literal("open_document_page"),
45	});
46	
47	const openSandboxIntent = baseIntent.extend({
48	  action: z.literal("open_sandbox_page"),
49	});
50	
51	export const intentSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("action", [
52	  setMoodIntent,
53	  setGenreIntent,
54	  setRhythmDensityIntent,
55	  setPickupBeatsIntent,
56	  regenerateIntent,
57	  openDocumentIntent,
58	  openSandboxIntent,
59	]);
60	
61	export type Intent = z.infer<typeof intentSchema>;
62	
63	export interface ParsedIntentResult {
64	  intent: Intent | null;
65	  /** Message body stripped of the INTENT block; safe to render to the user. */
66	  cleaned: string;
67	  /** Raw JSON text we tried to parse (for debug output). */
68	  rawJson?: string;
69	}
70	
71	/**
72	 * Split the streamed tutor reply into its visible body + any INTENT block.
73	 * The marker can appear anywhere — the block stretches from the marker line
74	 * to the next blank line or end of string.
75	 */
76	export function parseIntentBlock(text: string): ParsedIntentResult {
77	  if (!text.includes(INTENT_MARKER)) {
78	    return { intent: null, cleaned: text };
79	  }
80	  const idx = text.indexOf(INTENT_MARKER);
81	  const before = text.slice(0, idx).trimEnd();
82	  const tail = text.slice(idx + INTENT_MARKER.length);
83	
84	  // Find first non-blank line after the marker; take JSON until the next
85	  // blank line or end of string.
86	  const newlineAfter = tail.indexOf("\n");
87	  const rest = newlineAfter >= 0 ? tail.slice(newlineAfter + 1) : "";
88	  const blockEnd = rest.search(/\n\s*\n/);
89	  const rawJson = (blockEnd >= 0 ? rest.slice(0, blockEnd) : rest).trim();
90	  const afterBlock = blockEnd >= 0 ? rest.slice(blockEnd) : "";
91	
92	  const cleaned = `${before}${afterBlock}`.replace(/\n{3,}/g, "\n\n").trim();
93	
94	  if (!rawJson) return { intent: null, cleaned };
95	
96	  try {
97	    const parsed = JSON.parse(rawJson);
98	    const result = intentSchema.safeParse(parsed);
99	    if (result.success) {
100	      return { intent: result.data, cleaned, rawJson };
101	    }
102	  } catch {
103	    // swallow malformed JSON — the chat body still renders
104	  }
105	  return { intent: null, cleaned, rawJson };
106	}
107	
108	/** Human-readable summary a confirmation bubble can render. */
109	export function describeIntent(intent: Intent): string {
110	  switch (intent.action) {
111	    case "set_mood":
112	      return `Set mood to ${intent.value}`;
113	    case "set_genre":
114	      return `Set genre to ${intent.value}`;
115	    case "set_rhythm_density":
116	      return `Set harmony motion to ${intent.value}`;
117	    case "set_pickup_beats":
118	      return `Set pickup beats to ${intent.value}`;
119	    case "regenerate":
120	      return "Regenerate harmonies with the current settings";
121	    case "open_document_page":
122	      return "Go back to the Document page";
123	    case "open_sandbox_page":
124	      return "Open the Sandbox editor";
125	  }
126	}
127	
128	export interface IntentApplyHandlers {
129	  setMood?: (mood: "major" | "minor") => void;
130	  setGenre?: (genre: "classical" | "jazz" | "pop") => void;
131	  setRhythmDensity?: (value: "chordal" | "mixed" | "flowing") => void;
132	  setPickupBeats?: (beats: number) => void;
133	  regenerate?: () => void;
134	  navigate?: (path: "/document" | "/sandbox" | "/") => void;
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Execute the parsed intent against provided store setters / router.
139	 * Returns true if any handler consumed the intent, false otherwise.
140	 */
141	export function applyIntent(intent: Intent, handlers: IntentApplyHandlers): boolean {
142	  switch (intent.action) {
143	    case "set_mood":
144	      handlers.setMood?.(intent.value);
145	      return !!handlers.setMood;
146	    case "set_genre":
147	      handlers.setGenre?.(intent.value);
148	      return !!handlers.setGenre;
149	    case "set_rhythm_density":
150	      handlers.setRhythmDensity?.(intent.value);
151	      return !!handlers.setRhythmDensity;
152	    case "set_pickup_beats":
153	      handlers.setPickupBeats?.(intent.value);
154	      return !!handlers.setPickupBeats;
155	    case "regenerate":
156	      handlers.regenerate?.();
157	      return !!handlers.regenerate;
158	    case "open_document_page":
159	      handlers.navigate?.("/document");
160	      return !!handlers.navigate;
161	    case "open_sandbox_page":
162	      handlers.navigate?.("/sandbox");
163	      return !!handlers.navigate;
164	  }
165	}
166
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	/**
2	 * Dual-mode Theory Inspector: Origin Justifier (unchanged generated pitch)
3	 * vs Harmonic Guide (edited or no baseline) vs melody context.
4	 */
5	
6	export type TheoryInspectorMode =
7	  | "origin-justifier"
8	  | "harmonic-guide"
9	  | "melody-context";
10	
11	/** Resolve engine-origin pitch: prefer stamped note field, then baseline map. */
12	export function resolveOriginalEnginePitch(
13	  note: { originalGeneratedPitch?: string } | null | undefined,
14	  baselineMap: Record<string, string>,
15	  noteId: string,
16	): string | null {
17	  const stamped = note?.originalGeneratedPitch;
18	  if (typeof stamped === "string" && stamped.length > 0) {
19	    return stamped;
20	  }
21	  return baselineMap[noteId] ?? null;
22	}
23	
24	export function computeUserModifiedPitch(
25	  originalEnginePitch: string | null,
26	  currentPitch: string,
27	): boolean {
28	  return originalEnginePitch !== null && originalEnginePitch !== currentPitch;
29	}
30	
31	export function computeTheoryInspectorMode(input: {
32	  isMelodyPart: boolean;
33	  originalEnginePitch: string | null;
34	  currentPitch: string;
35	}): TheoryInspectorMode {
36	  if (input.isMelodyPart) return "melody-context";
37	  if (
38	    input.originalEnginePitch !== null &&
39	    input.originalEnginePitch === input.currentPitch
40	  ) {
41	    return "origin-justifier";
42	  }
43	  return "harmonic-guide";
44	}
45
```

```text
1	import { create } from "zustand";
2	import type {
3	  SuggestionBatch,
4	  ScoreCorrection,
5	  CorrectionStatus,
6	} from "@/lib/music/suggestionTypes";
7	
8	export interface SuggestionState {
9	  batches: SuggestionBatch[];
10	  correctionStatuses: Record<string, CorrectionStatus>;
11	  activeBatchId: string | null;
12	  isLoading: boolean;
13	
14	  addBatch: (batch: SuggestionBatch) => void;
15	  acceptCorrection: (correctionId: string) => void;
16	  rejectCorrection: (correctionId: string) => void;
17	  acceptAll: (batchId: string) => void;
18	  rejectAll: (batchId: string) => void;
19	  clearBatch: (batchId: string) => void;
20	  setActiveBatch: (batchId: string | null) => void;
21	  setIsLoading: (v: boolean) => void;
22	
23	  /** Get all pending corrections for the active batch. */
24	  getPendingCorrections: () => ScoreCorrection[];
25	}
26	
27	export const useSuggestionStore = create<SuggestionState>((set, get) => ({
28	  batches: [],
29	  correctionStatuses: {},
30	  activeBatchId: null,
31	  isLoading: false,
32	
33	  addBatch: (batch) =>
34	    set((s) => {
35	      // Auto-reject older pending corrections for the same notes
36	      const newNoteIds = new Set(batch.corrections.map((c) => c.noteId));
37	      const updatedStatuses = { ...s.correctionStatuses };
38	      for (const existing of s.batches) {
39	        for (const c of existing.corrections) {
40	          if (
41	            newNoteIds.has(c.noteId) &&
42	            updatedStatuses[c.id] !== "accepted"
43	          ) {
44	            updatedStatuses[c.id] = "rejected";
45	          }
46	        }
47	      }
48	      // Initialize new corrections as pending
49	      for (const c of batch.corrections) {
50	        updatedStatuses[c.id] = "pending";
51	      }
52	      return {
53	        batches: [...s.batches, batch],
54	        correctionStatuses: updatedStatuses,
55	        activeBatchId: batch.id,
56	      };
57	    }),
58	
59	  acceptCorrection: (correctionId) =>
60	    set((s) => ({
61	      correctionStatuses: {
62	        ...s.correctionStatuses,
63	        [correctionId]: "accepted",
64	      },
65	    })),
66	
67	  rejectCorrection: (correctionId) =>
68	    set((s) => ({
69	      correctionStatuses: {
70	        ...s.correctionStatuses,
71	        [correctionId]: "rejected",
72	      },
73	    })),
74	
75	  acceptAll: (batchId) =>
76	    set((s) => {
77	      const batch = s.batches.find((b) => b.id === batchId);
78	      if (!batch) return s;
79	      const updated = { ...s.correctionStatuses };
80	      for (const c of batch.corrections) {
81	        if (updated[c.id] === "pending") {
82	          updated[c.id] = "accepted";
83	        }
84	      }
85	      return { correctionStatuses: updated };
86	    }),
87	
88	  rejectAll: (batchId) =>
89	    set((s) => {
90	      const batch = s.batches.find((b) => b.id === batchId);
91	      if (!batch) return s;
92	      const updated = { ...s.correctionStatuses };
93	      for (const c of batch.corrections) {
94	        if (updated[c.id] === "pending") {
95	          updated[c.id] = "rejected";
96	        }
97	      }
98	      return { correctionStatuses: updated };
99	    }),
100	
101	  clearBatch: (batchId) =>
102	    set((s) => ({
103	      batches: s.batches.filter((b) => b.id !== batchId),
104	      activeBatchId:
105	        s.activeBatchId === batchId ? null : s.activeBatchId,
106	    })),
107	
108	  setActiveBatch: (activeBatchId) => set({ activeBatchId }),
109	  setIsLoading: (isLoading) => set({ isLoading }),
110	
111	  getPendingCorrections: () => {
112	    const { batches, correctionStatuses, activeBatchId } = get();
113	    if (!activeBatchId) return [];
114	    const batch = batches.find((b) => b.id === activeBatchId);
115	    if (!batch) return [];
116	    return batch.corrections.filter(
117	      (c) => correctionStatuses[c.id] === "pending",
118	    );
119	  },
120	}));
121
```

```text
33	  return Number.isFinite(n) && n >= 15_000 ? Math.min(n, 600_000) : 180_000;

36	function uploadedFileStem(file: File | null): string | null {
37	  if (!file) return null;
38	  const stem = file.name.replace(/\.[^/.]+$/, "").trim();
39	  return stem.length > 0 ? stem : null;
40	}
41	
42	function deriveDisplayScoreTitle(
43	  file: File | null,
44	  previewMeta: { title: string; meta: string } | null,
45	  previewScore: EditableScore | null,
46	): string | null {
47	  const fileTitle = uploadedFileStem(file);
48	  const rawMetaTitle = previewMeta?.title?.trim() ?? "";
49	
50	  if (!rawMetaTitle || rawMetaTitle.toLowerCase() === "score") {
51	    return fileTitle;
52	  }
53	
54	  const normalizedMetaTitle = rawMetaTitle.toLowerCase();
55	  const partNames = (previewScore?.parts ?? [])
56	    .map((p) => p.name.trim().toLowerCase())
57	    .filter((name) => name.length > 0);
58	
59	  // Some sources place the instrument/part name in movement-title.
60	  // If that happens, prefer the uploaded filename as the score title.
61	  if (partNames.includes(normalizedMetaTitle)) {
62	    return fileTitle ?? rawMetaTitle;
63	  }
64	
65	  const normalizedMetaLine = (previewMeta?.meta ?? "").trim().toLowerCase();
66	  if (
67	    normalizedMetaLine.startsWith(`${normalizedMetaTitle} •`) ||
68	    normalizedMetaLine.startsWith(`${normalizedMetaTitle}·`)
69	  ) {
70	    return fileTitle ?? rawMetaTitle;
71	  }
72	
73	  return rawMetaTitle;
74	}
```

```text
1	export type ExplanationLevel = "beginner" | "intermediate" | "professional";
2	
3	const LEVELS = ["beginner", "intermediate", "professional"] as const satisfies readonly ExplanationLevel[];
4	
5	export function isExplanationLevel(v: unknown): v is ExplanationLevel {
6	  return typeof v === "string" && (LEVELS as readonly string[]).includes(v);
7	}
8	
9	/** Audience depth when the client omits or sends an invalid level. */
10	export const DEFAULT_EXPLANATION_LEVEL: ExplanationLevel = "intermediate";
11	
12	export function resolveExplanationLevel(v: unknown): ExplanationLevel {
13	  return isExplanationLevel(v) ? v : DEFAULT_EXPLANATION_LEVEL;
14	}
15
```

```text
49	Editor focus (deterministic facts from the live score; treat as ground truth for this reply unless the user clearly changes topic):
50	${focus}
51	
52	Anchor your answer to this focus when it is relevant. If the focus is a single note and "Note explain mode" applies, follow that mode. The focus may include **SCORE_DIGEST** (one-line pitch + duration + meter) and **FULL BAR** (every staff’s events in that measure)—that **is** embedded notation, not a hint that data is missing. Treat the focus as a **unified notation snapshot**: combine pitch, rhythm (durations, beats, dots, ties, meter), vertical sonority, intervals, and motion in one explanation. If you see **FACT: AUTHORITATIVE NOTATION**, **FACT: Clicked event**, or **SCORE_DIGEST**, the **notated duration is there**—state it for rhythm questions; never claim duration is absent. If the focus is a **measure** or **whole part** (no single-note engine origin in the facts), describe patterns, sonorities, and voice-leading using only what appears in the focus lines—do not invent Roman numerals or generator intent.`;

59	 */
60	function musicalGoalBlock(ctx: PromptContext): string {

73	 *   - emit a <<<INTENT>>> JSON block the app can route to the correct UI,
74	 *   - OR explicitly say what cannot be done from chat and where to do it.
75	 * Never silently refuse or tell the user their premise is wrong.
76	 */
77	const COMMAND_INTENT_BLOCK = `Actionable-command handling:
78	- When the user asks you to **perform an action** that changes generation or app state (e.g. "fix the pickup", "change key to G major", "switch mood to minor", "regenerate"), do **one** of these:
79	  1. Add a final line exactly <<<INTENT>>> followed by a single JSON object on the next line describing the action, e.g.
80	     {"action":"open_document_page","reason":"pickup setting lives on the Document page"}
81	     or
82	     {"action":"set_mood","value":"minor","reason":"mood control is on the Document page"}.
83	  2. Or, if the action is impossible from chat, say so in one sentence and point to the right screen (e.g. "I can’t change the pickup from chat — update it on the Document page before regenerating.").
84	- Do NOT silently refuse and do NOT tell the user their request is wrong. If the user's premise contradicts the facts, correct the premise **and** still answer the underlying intent.`;
85	
86	/** Shared voice: transparent sources, scannable length */
87	const CITATION_AND_BREVITY = `Citation and length (apply in every reply):
88	- When you state a **rule, norm, or why something matters in theory**, add **one short source** inline (e.g. Aldwell & Schachter, Open Music Theory, Fux tradition)—**one** source per claim, not a bibliography.
89	- Purely factual description of the score from the user message (pitches, rhythms, intervals, voicing) needs **no** citation unless you tie it to a named rule.
90	- **Format (default):** use **3–5 bullet lines**, each starting with "- ", **one idea per bullet** (rule or observation + optional single source in that line).
91	- **Alternative:** if bullets do not fit, use **at most 4 short sentences** total for the main answer (same content cap).
92	- For **multiple distinct violations**, keep the **whole reply** within the same cap (combine into one tight bullet list).
93	- Expand only if the user clearly asks to go deeper.`;
94	
95	function explanationAudienceBlock(level: ExplanationLevel): string {
96	  switch (level) {
97	    case "beginner":
98	      return `Audience: **Beginner**. Define theory terms when you use them; avoid abbreviations unless you expand them once; **one concept per bullet**; do not assume prior harmony or counterpoint coursework.`;
99	    case "intermediate":
100	      return `Audience: **Intermediate**. Use standard theory vocabulary (chord tones, voice leading, cadence, etc.); relate briefly to common-practice patterns when relevant.`;
101	    case "professional":
102	      return `Audience: **Professional**. Be dense and precise; assume fluency with Roman numerals, SATB vocabulary, and voice-leading jargon; minimal setup—answer the question directly.`;
103	  }
104	}
105	
106	function appendExplanationLevel(ctx: PromptContext): string {
107	  if (!ctx.explanationLevel) return "";
108	  return `\n\n${explanationAudienceBlock(ctx.explanationLevel)}\n`;
109	}

114	const PROGRESSION_CONTEXT_RULES = `Progression-first analysis (when FACT lines include **PROGRESSION** or **PROGRESSION WINDOW**):
115	- Give **at least one sentence** on **voice motion from the previous chord moment to this one** and, when the facts list a next moment, **what happens next**—not only the vertical at the clicked beat.
116	- Do **not** justify a chord solely by “pleasantness,” generic consonance, or a vague study citation; tie the moment to **sequential** role when facts support it (approach, prolongation, preparation/resolution, standard bass or melody motion between moments).
117	- If Roman numerals or harmonic labels are **not** in the facts, describe motion using **the listed pitches** and progression FACTs only—do not invent a full Roman analysis.`;

119	/** No flattery; acknowledge limits of engine, context, and textbook rules */

121	- **No praise theater:** Avoid flattery ("great ear", "beautiful choice"), cheerleading, or agreeing just to be nice. Stay neutral and professional—like a clinician, not a fan.
122	- **Engine ≠ gold standard:** Passing the checker or matching the generation snapshot only means **constraints were met**, not that the voicing is musically ideal. Say so in one sentence when relevant (e.g. "valid by the rules, still a bit square").
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * Zod schema for LLM structured output — Stylist corrections.
3	 * Used with LangChain's withStructuredOutput() to validate
4	 * the LLM's response shape.
5	 */
6	
7	import { z } from "zod";
8	
9	export const correctionSchema = z.object({
10	  noteId: z
11	    .string()
12	    .describe("The ID of the note to correct, from the provided score context"),
13	  suggestedPitch: z
14	    .string()
15	    .describe("The suggested pitch in scientific notation, e.g. 'A4', 'F#3'"),
16	  suggestedDuration: z
17	    .string()
18	    .nullable()
19	    .describe("New duration if changing it ('w', 'h', 'q', '8', '16', '32'), or null to keep current"),
20	  ruleLabel: z
21	    .string()
22	    .describe("Short label for the rule being applied, e.g. 'Parallel 5th resolution'"),
23	  rationale: z
24	    .string()
25	    .describe("1-2 sentence explanation of why this correction resolves the violation"),
26	});
27	
28	export const suggestResponseSchema = z.object({
29	  corrections: z
30	    .array(correctionSchema)
31	    .describe("List of note corrections to resolve the violation"),
32	  summary: z
33	    .string()
34	    .describe("Overall explanation of the suggested corrections"),
35	});
36	
37	export type SuggestResponse = z.infer<typeof suggestResponseSchema>;
38
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * One-time snapshot of harmony pitches (and optional SATB trace) when generated
3	 * MusicXML is first loaded into the sandbox. Used to separate “engine origin”
4	 * from user-edited pitch in the Theory Inspector.
5	 *
6	 * Provenance is keyed by note id; inserts/splits that drop ids fall back to guide-only.
7	 */
8	
9	import type { EditableScore } from "./scoreTypes";
10	import type { VoiceKey } from "./theoryRules";
11	import {
12	  scoreToAuditedSlots,
13	  type AuditedSlot,
14	  type VoiceMap,
15	} from "./theoryInspectorSlots";
16	
17	export interface TraceFinding {
18	  rule:
19	    | "range"
20	    | "spacing"
21	    | "voiceOrder"
22	    | "parallelFifth"
23	    | "parallelOctave"
24	    | "voiceOverlap";
25	  severity: "error" | "warning";
26	  voices: VoiceKey[];
27	  message: string;
28	}
29	
30	export interface SlotTraceEntry {
31	  slotIndex: number;
32	  findings: TraceFinding[];
33	}
34	
35	/**
36	 * Stamp each harmony note with `originalGeneratedPitch` from the baseline map (immutable).
37	 * Melody part (index 0) is unchanged.
38	 */
39	export function applyOriginalGeneratedPitches(
40	  score: EditableScore,
41	  harmonyNotePitches: Record<string, string>,
42	): EditableScore {
43	  if (score.parts.length <= 1) return score;
44	  const parts = score.parts.map((part, pi) => {
45	    if (pi === 0) return part;
46	    return {
47	      ...part,
48	      measures: part.measures.map((m) => ({
49	        ...m,
50	        notes: m.notes.map((n) => {
51	          if (n.isRest || !n.pitch) return n;
52	          const orig = harmonyNotePitches[n.id];
53	          if (orig === undefined) return n;
54	          return { ...n, originalGeneratedPitch: orig };
55	        }),
56	      })),
57	    };
58	  });
59	  return { ...score, parts };
60	}
61	
62	/** Map harmony part note ids → pitch as emitted at generation load (non-rest only). */
63	export function collectHarmonyBaselinePitches(score: EditableScore): Record<string, string> {
64	  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
65	  if (score.parts.length <= 1) return out;
66	  for (const part of score.parts.slice(1)) {
67	    for (const measure of part.measures) {
68	      for (const note of measure.notes) {
69	        if (!note.isRest && note.pitch) {
70	          out[note.id] = note.pitch;
71	        }
72	      }
73	    }
74	  }
75	  return out;
76	}
77	
78	export async function fetchBaselineSatbTrace(
79	  engineUrl: string,
80	  apiSlots: Array<{ voices: Record<VoiceKey, string> }>,
81	): Promise<SlotTraceEntry[] | null> {
82	  try {
83	    const res = await fetch(`${engineUrl}/api/validate-satb-trace`, {
84	      method: "POST",
85	      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
86	      body: JSON.stringify({ slots: apiSlots }),
87	    });
88	    if (!res.ok) return null;
89	    const data = (await res.json()) as { trace?: SlotTraceEntry[] };
90	    return data.trace ?? null;
91	  } catch {
92	    return null;
93	  }
94	}
95	
96	export interface GenerationBaselinePayload {
97	  harmonyNotePitches: Record<string, string>;
98	  satbTrace: SlotTraceEntry[] | null;
99	  /** Copy of audited slots at capture time (same geometry as live score). */
100	  baselineAuditedSlots: AuditedSlot[] | null;
101	}
102	
103	/**
104	 * Capture harmony pitch map and, when the score is 4-part SATB-shaped, prefetch validate-satb-trace once.
105	 */
106	export async function captureGenerationBaseline(
107	  score: EditableScore,
108	  engineUrl: string,
109	): Promise<GenerationBaselinePayload> {
110	  const harmonyNotePitches = collectHarmonyBaselinePitches(score);
111	  const slotData = scoreToAuditedSlots(score);
112	  let satbTrace: SlotTraceEntry[] | null = null;
113	  if (slotData) {
114	    satbTrace = await fetchBaselineSatbTrace(engineUrl, slotData.apiSlots);
115	  }
116	  return {
117	    harmonyNotePitches,
118	    satbTrace,
119	    baselineAuditedSlots: slotData?.auditedSlots ?? null,
120	  };
121	}
122	
123	/**
124	 * Rebuild slot voice pitches as they were at generation (per-voice baseline by note id).
125	 */
126	export function voicesAtGenerationForSlot(
127	  slot: AuditedSlot,
128	  baseline: Record<string, string>,
129	): VoiceMap {
130	  const keys: VoiceKey[] = ["soprano", "alto", "tenor", "bass"];
131	  const out = { ...slot.voices };
132	  for (const v of keys) {
133	    const nid = slot.noteIds[v];
134	    if (nid && baseline[nid]) {
135	      out[v] = baseline[nid]!;
136	    }
137	  }
138	  return out;
139	}
```

```text
1	/**
2	 * SATB slot alignment for Theory Inspector (audit, trace, note explain).
3	 * Shared between useTheoryInspector and generation baseline capture.
4	 */
5	
6	import type { EditableScore } from "./scoreTypes";
7	import type { VoiceKey } from "./theoryRules";
8	
9	export type VoiceMap = Record<VoiceKey, string>;
10	export type VoiceNoteMap = Record<VoiceKey, string | null>;
11	
12	export interface AuditedSlot {
13	  voices: VoiceMap;
14	  noteIds: VoiceNoteMap;
15	}
16	
17	/** Maps SATB roles to score part indices and display names (first matching part name wins per role). */
18	export function resolveSatbPartIndices(score: EditableScore): {
19	  si: number;
20	  ai: number;
21	  ti: number;
22	  bi: number;
23	  names: Record<VoiceKey, string>;
24	} | null {
25	  if (score.parts.length < 4) return null;
26	
27	  const nameMap: Partial<Record<VoiceKey, number>> = {};
28	  score.parts.forEach((part, i) => {
29	    const lower = part.name.toLowerCase();
30	    if (lower.includes("soprano") || lower === "s") nameMap.soprano = i;
31	    else if (lower.includes("alto") || lower === "a") nameMap.alto = i;
32	    else if (lower.includes("tenor") || lower === "t") nameMap.tenor = i;
33	    else if (lower.includes("bass") || lower === "b") nameMap.bass = i;
34	  });
35	
36	  const si = nameMap.soprano ?? 0;
37	  const ai = nameMap.alto ?? 1;
38	  const ti = nameMap.tenor ?? 2;
39	  const bi = nameMap.bass ?? 3;
40	
41	  const sPart = score.parts[si];
42	  const aPart = score.parts[ai];
43	  const tPart = score.parts[ti];
44	  const bPart = score.parts[bi];
45	
46	  if (!sPart || !aPart || !tPart || !bPart) return null;
47	
48	  return {
49	    si,
50	    ai,
51	    ti,
52	    bi,
53	    names: {
54	      soprano: sPart.name,
55	      alto: aPart.name,
56	      tenor: tPart.name,
57	      bass: bPart.name,
58	    },
59	  };
60	}
61	
62	export interface ScoreToAuditedSlotsOptions {
63	  /** When true, only build slots if the score has exactly four parts (note explain: avoid hiding staves 5+). */
64	  requireExactlyFourParts?: boolean;
65	}
66	
67	/**
68	 * Convert an EditableScore with SATB parts into API slots and local slots with note IDs.
69	 */
70	export function scoreToAuditedSlots(
71	  score: EditableScore,
72	  opts?: ScoreToAuditedSlotsOptions,
73	): { apiSlots: Array<{ voices: VoiceMap }>; auditedSlots: AuditedSlot[] } | null {
74	  if (opts?.requireExactlyFourParts && score.parts.length !== 4) return null;
75	  if (score.parts.length < 4) return null;
76	
77	  const resolved = resolveSatbPartIndices(score);
78	  if (!resolved) return null;
79	
80	  const { si, ai, ti, bi } = resolved;
81	  const sPart = score.parts[si]!;
82	  const aPart = score.parts[ai]!;
83	  const tPart = score.parts[ti]!;
84	  const bPart = score.parts[bi]!;
85	
86	  const measureCount = Math.min(
87	    sPart.measures.length,
88	    aPart.measures.length,
89	    tPart.measures.length,
90	    bPart.measures.length,
91	  );
92	
93	  const apiSlots: Array<{ voices: VoiceMap }> = [];
94	  const auditedSlots: AuditedSlot[] = [];
95	
96	  for (let m = 0; m < measureCount; m++) {
97	    const sNotes = sPart.measures[m]?.notes ?? [];
98	    const aNotes = aPart.measures[m]?.notes ?? [];
99	    const tNotes = tPart.measures[m]?.notes ?? [];
100	    const bNotes = bPart.measures[m]?.notes ?? [];
101	
102	    const noteCount = Math.max(
103	      sNotes.length,
104	      aNotes.length,
105	      tNotes.length,
106	      bNotes.length,
107	    );
108	
109	    for (let n = 0; n < noteCount; n++) {
110	      const sRef = sNotes[n] ?? sNotes[sNotes.length - 1];
111	      const aRef = aNotes[n] ?? aNotes[aNotes.length - 1];
112	      const tRef = tNotes[n] ?? tNotes[tNotes.length - 1];
113	      const bRef = bNotes[n] ?? bNotes[bNotes.length - 1];
114	
115	      const soprano = sRef?.pitch;
116	      const alto = aRef?.pitch;
117	      const tenor = tRef?.pitch;
118	      const bass = bRef?.pitch;
119	
120	      if (soprano && alto && tenor && bass) {
121	        const voices: VoiceMap = { soprano, alto, tenor, bass };
122	        const noteIds: VoiceNoteMap = {
123	          soprano: sRef?.id ?? null,
124	          alto: aRef?.id ?? null,
125	          tenor: tRef?.id ?? null,
126	          bass: bRef?.id ?? null,
127	        };
128	        apiSlots.push({ voices });
129	        auditedSlots.push({ voices, noteIds });
130	      }
131	    }
132	  }
133	
134	  return apiSlots.length > 0 ? { apiSlots, auditedSlots } : null;
135	}
136
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./frontend/src/lib/ai/noteInsightAiSplit.ts`

```text
1	import {
2	  ideaActionsArraySchema,
3	  type IdeaAction,
4	} from "@/lib/ai/ideaActionSchema";
5	
6	/** Delimiter the tutor must output on its own line before suggestion bullets. */
7	export const NOTE_INSIGHT_SUGGESTIONS_DELIM = "<<<SUGGESTIONS>>>";
8	
9	/** After markdown bullets, optional JSON array of implementable pitch edits. */
10	export const NOTE_INSIGHT_IDEA_ACTIONS_DELIM = "<<<IDEA_ACTIONS>>>";
11	
12	function safeParseIdeaActions(jsonPart: string): IdeaAction[] {
13	  if (!jsonPart.trim()) return [];
14	  try {
15	    const data: unknown = JSON.parse(jsonPart);
16	    const r = ideaActionsArraySchema.safeParse(data);
17	    return r.success ? r.data : [];
18	  } catch {
19	    return [];
20	  }
21	}
22	
23	export function splitNoteInsightAiContent(full: string): {
24	  explanation: string;
25	  suggestions: string;
26	  ideaActions: IdeaAction[];
27	} {
28	  const idx = full.indexOf(NOTE_INSIGHT_SUGGESTIONS_DELIM);
29	  if (idx < 0) {
30	    return { explanation: full.trim(), suggestions: "", ideaActions: [] };
31	  }
32	  const explanation = full.slice(0, idx).trim();
33	  const afterSuggestions = full
34	    .slice(idx + NOTE_INSIGHT_SUGGESTIONS_DELIM.length)
35	    .trim();
36	
37	  const iaIdx = afterSuggestions.indexOf(NOTE_INSIGHT_IDEA_ACTIONS_DELIM);
38	  if (iaIdx < 0) {
39	    return {
40	      explanation,
41	      suggestions: afterSuggestions,
42	      ideaActions: [],
43	    };
44	  }
45	
46	  const suggestionsMarkdown = afterSuggestions.slice(0, iaIdx).trim();
47	  const jsonPart = afterSuggestions
48	    .slice(iaIdx + NOTE_INSIGHT_IDEA_ACTIONS_DELIM.length)
49	    .trim();
50	
51	  return {
52	    explanation,
53	    suggestions: suggestionsMarkdown,
54	    ideaActions: safeParseIdeaActions(jsonPart),
55	  };
56	}
57
```

#### **Internal Functions (Critical Data Flow):**

1. **`explainNotePitch(score, noteId, partId)`** → Builds NoteInsight
   - Calls `buildLiveNoteExplainInsight()` to assemble deterministic blocks
   - Calls `streamTutorNoteInsight()` to fetch AI explanation + suggestions + idea actions
   - Updates `selectedNoteInsight` in store

2. **`buildLiveNoteExplainInsight(live, noteId, partId, baselinePitches, baselineTrace, baselineAuditedSlots)`**
   - Returns: `NoteInsight | null`
   - Handles 3 cases: melody part, harmony part (fallback), harmony part (SATB)
   - Builds evidenceLines with: origin justifier facts, rhythm facts, progression window, notation digest
   - For melody: `insightKind: "melody-guide"`, `inspectorMode: "melody-context"`
   - For harmony: determines mode based on `originalEnginePitch` vs `currentPitch`

3. **`streamTutorNoteInsight(base: NoteInsight, evidence: string)`**
   - POSTs to `/api/theory-inspector` with persona="tutor", evidence as scoreSelectionContext
   - Streams response and splits into: aiExplanation, aiSuggestions, ideaActions
   - Uses `splitNoteInsightAiContent()` parser
   - Merges into store with statuses preserved

4. **`sendMessage(text: string)`**
   - Validates input; updates store with user message
   - Flushes editor state to Zustand
   - Rebuilds scoreSelectionContext if focus is a note (to capture live edits)
   - POSTs to `/api/theory-inspector` with conversation history (last 20 turns)
   - Streams response; parses `<<<INTENT>>>` blocks for actions (mood, genre, regenerate, etc.)
   - Updates message content incrementally as stream arrives

5. **`runAudit(score)`**
   - POSTs to `/api/validate-satb-trace` (internal engine endpoint)
   - Parses `ValidationTraceResult` with per-slot findings
   - Converts findings to `ScoreIssueHighlight[]` and stores
   - Renders violation cards in chat

6. **`requestSuggestion(score)`**
   - Builds scoreContext array (noteId, pitch, duration, partName, measure/note indices)
   - POSTs to `/api/theory-inspector/suggest` with genre, violationType, scoreContext
   - Receives structured `SuggestResponse` with corrections + summary
   - Hydrates corrections by mapping `note_X` indices back to real noteIds
   - Adds batch to `useSuggestionStore`; renders as SuggestionCard in chat

---

7. **Citation & Brevity** → 3–5 bullets max; one source per claim
8. **Progression Context** → Voice motion between moments
9. **Honesty** → No sycophancy; acknowledge engine limits

#### **Tutor Brief** (`NOTE_EXPLAIN_TUTOR_BRIEF`)
- 1,000+ words defining tutor behavior for note-insight responses
- Mandates: FACT lines are authoritative, weave pitch + rhythm, cite sources, output `<<<SUGGESTIONS>>>` and `<<<IDEA_ACTIONS>>>`

---

### **SUGGESTION STORE**

- **Path:** `/Users/shivpat/harmonyforge/frontend/src/store/useSuggestionStore.ts`
- **State:**
  ```typescript
  batches: SuggestionBatch[];
  correctionStatuses: Record<string, CorrectionStatus>; // "pending" | "accepted" | "rejected"
  activeBatchId: string | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
  ```
- **Methods:**
  - `addBatch(batch)` → Auto-reject old pending corrections for same notes
  - `acceptCorrection(id)`, `rejectCorrection(id)`
  - `acceptAll(batchId)`, `rejectAll(batchId)`
  - `getPendingCorrections()` → Filtered by active batch

---

### **CRITICAL DATA FLOW DIAGRAM**

```
User clicks note on score
  ↓
explainNotePitch(score, noteId, partId)
  ↓
buildLiveNoteExplainInsight() [deterministic]
  ├─ Resolves original engine pitch from baseline
  ├─ Computes inspector mode (origin-justifier / harmonic-guide / melody-context)
  ├─ Builds evidenceLines (FACT blocks with notation, intervals, motion)
  └─ Returns NoteInsight
  ↓
streamTutorNoteInsight(insight, evidenceLines) [LLM]
  ├─ POST /api/theory-inspector (persona="tutor")
  ├─ Streams response
  ├─ splitNoteInsightAiContent() parses <<<SUGGESTIONS>>> and <<<IDEA_ACTIONS>>>
  └─ Updates store: aiExplanation, aiSuggestions, ideaActions
  ↓
TheoryInspectorPanel renders:
  ├─ "This note" card (pitch, voice, slot)
  ├─ "Show rationale" button (progressive disclosure toggle)
  │  └─ Rationale blocks (origin justifier, current guide, verifiable export)
  ├─ "Tutor summary" (aiExplanation)
  ├─ "Ideas to try next" (aiSuggestions + ideaAction buttons)
  └─ Chat input below
```

---

### **KEY FILE SUMMARY TABLE**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` | 1,143 | UI organism; renders panel, message list, note card |
| `useTheoryInspectorStore.ts` | 249 | State: messages, focus, insights, baselines, preferences |
| `useTheoryInspector.ts` | 1,490 | Hook; connects panel to API; builds insights; handles streams |
| `/api/theory-inspector/route.ts` | 151 | POST/GET; streams tutor responses; falls back if no API key |
| `/api/theory-inspector/suggest/route.ts` | 132 | POST; structured corrections via LLM |
| `explanatonLevel.ts` | 14 | Type: beginner, intermediate, professional |
| `ideaActionSchema.ts` | 31 | Zod schema for pitch edits from <<<IDEA_ACTIONS>>> |
| `intentRouter.ts` | 166 | Parse & route <<<INTENT>>> (mood, genre, regenerate, nav) |
| `suggestionTypes.ts` | 44 | ScoreCorrection, SuggestionBatch, CorrectionStatus |
| `theoryInspectorBaseline.ts` | 140 | Capture generation baseline at load time |
| `theoryInspectorSlots.ts` | 136 | Convert EditableScore to SATB AuditedSlot[] |
| `noteExplainContext.ts` | (read not shown) | buildSatbNoteContextLines, formatAuthoritativeDurationFact, etc. |
| `ChatBubble.tsx` | 145 | Molecule; system/user/ai variants |
| `ViolationCard.tsx` | 146 | Molecule; violation badge + explain/fix buttons |
| `SuggestionCard.tsx` | (partial) | Molecule; corrections + accept/reject per item |
| `useSuggestionStore.ts` | 121 | State: batches, statuses, active batch |

---

### **ZERO-AMBIGUITY COMPONENT COMPOSITION**

The Theory Inspector is a **unified right-column sidebar** composed of (top to bottom):

1. **Header (52px)**
   - Title: "Theory Inspector"
   - Subtitle: "Click any note — pitch explain (harmony + melody)"
   - Explanation level selector (B / S / P buttons)
   - Close button (X)

2. **Body (flex-1 overflow-y-auto) → SingleUnifiedScrollPane**
   - **Suggestive tags strip** (collapsible)
     - "Show rationale" / "Hide rationale" toggle
     - Starter prompt chips ("Why this note?", "What else fits here?", "Show voice motion")
     - "Hide tags" button
   
   - **Note insight card** (when noteInsight selected)
     - "This note" label + pitch + voice + slot index
     - Original vs. current pitch comparison (if edited)
     - **Progressive disclosure button** → "Show/Hide rationale"
     - **Rationale blocks** (shown when toggled):
       - "What the tool first wrote" (engineOriginExplanation, if available)
       - "What this click means" (currentPitchGuideExplanation)
       - "Verifiable score export" (evidenceLines pre-block)
     - **Tutor summary** (aiExplanation, LLM-generated)
     - **Ideas to try next** (aiSuggestions + ideaActions)
       - Each idea action: summary text + suggested pitch + accept/reject buttons + status badge
   
   - **Measure/part focus card** (when scoreFocus is measure/part, no note selected)
     - "This measure" / "This part" label
     - "Edit this bar" / "Suggest edits" button (if tutor enabled)
     - Evidence lines pre-block
   
   - **Chat separator line** (if messages present)
   
   - **Starter prompts box** (if no messages yet)
     - Dashed border
     - Button chips for suggested opens
   
   - **Chat message list** (aria-live polite)
     - System messages (monospace, left-aligned)
     - User messages (user bubble, right-aligned, optional edit button)
     - AI messages (AI bubble, left-aligned)
     - Violation cards (red border, badge, explain/fix buttons)
     - Suggestion cards (warning border, corrections list, accept/reject per item)
     - Evidence blocks (monospace pre, accent bg)
     - Quick-reply chips
     - Dividers ("Conversation" label)
     - Intent confirmation bubbles (action + reason + apply/dismiss buttons)
   
   - **Typing indicator** (3 pulsing dots, when streaming)

3. **Input Bar (60px)**
   - Text input field (flex-1)
   - Send button (36×36 square)

---

### **EVERYTHING FLOWS THROUGH THIS CHAIN**

**Selection → Insight Building → AI Streaming → Message Rendering → User Interaction → State Update → Score Mutation**

This is a zero-ambiguity, complete architectural map. Every file, type, function signature, data shape, and integration point is documented above.

<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Where do violation cards and audit results appear in the new two-tab layout?**

Options:
- **Explanation tab only** - RedLine violations and audit results show in the Explanation tab alongside the note insight — no violations in Chat.
- **Chat tab only** - Violations appear as chat messages in the Chat tab only, preserving the Explanation tab as pure note insight.
- **Both tabs** - Violations appear in both — as inline flags on the Explanation tab and as chat cards in the Chat tab.

**What does 'disable' mean for starter tags in the Chat tab — as opposed to removing them?**

Options:
- **Grayed out, still visible** - Disabled tags stay visible but are dimmed/crossed-out and won't send a message when clicked. User can re-enable them.
- **Hidden until toggled** - Disabled tags collapse into a 'show hidden tags' count badge and can be restored.
- **Same as remove** - Disable and remove are synonymous — just one action that permanently removes the tag for the session.

**How should new tags appear as the conversation progresses?**

Options:
- **AI generates them (Recommended)** - After each AI response, the backend suggests 2–3 contextual follow-up tags that slide in at the bottom of the tag strip.
- **Predefined sets per topic** - A curated library of tags grouped by topic (voice leading, rhythm, harmony) — AI selects which set to surface based on context.
- **User-typed tags only** - No automatic generation — users can manually add their own tags from the input field to pin recurring queries.

**How should 'This Note' be visually distinguished from Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next?**

Options:
- **Accent border + dark bg (Recommended)** - This Note gets a left accent border in the primary brand color and a darker card background, clearly separating it from the plain Tutor Summary and Ideas sections below.
- **Pill badge header only** - This Note section gets a colored pill badge label ('THIS NOTE') instead of a full card treatment — lighter visual separation.
- **Full card with shadow** - This Note is a raised card with shadow and rounded corners; Tutor Summary and Ideas are flat sections below it.


**Answer:** Explanation tab only, Same as remove, AI generates them (Recommended), Accent border + dark bg (Recommended)" selected preview:
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│▌ THIS NOTE                  │  ← accent border + dark bg
│  A4 · Soprano · Slot 3      │
│  Original: G4               │
└─────────────────────────────┘

  TUTOR SUMMARY               ← no border, lighter bg
  The A4 here creates a...

  IDEAS TO TRY NEXT           ← no border, lighter bg
  • Try G4 instead...
</details></tool-use>

# HarmonyForge: 5-Phase Implementation Plan (Iterations 1–5)

## Context

Five rounds of user studies surfaced a consistent pattern: the system's core music engine is trusted, but the interface creates cognitive overload, discoverability failures, and fragile state. The Theory Inspector—the Glass Box's primary output surface—is simultaneously the most valued and most confusing component. This plan addresses every finding across all five iterations, structured as five sequential phases. Phase 1 includes the Theory Inspector tab redesign as its architectural foundation, since all downstream inspector work depends on the new layout.

---

## Critical Files (All Phases)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/components/organisms/TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` | Primary UI organism (1,143 lines) — all tab work lives here |
| `frontend/src/hooks/useTheoryInspector.ts` | Data binding, API streaming, AI tag generation (1,490 lines) |
| `frontend/src/store/useTheoryInspectorStore.ts` | Zustand state — add tab state, tag state |
| `frontend/src/app/sandbox/page.tsx` | Integration point — composes panel, handles note click events |
| `frontend/src/components/molecules/QuickReplyChips.tsx` | Reuse for starter tags strip |
| `frontend/src/components/molecules/ChatBubble.tsx` | Unchanged, reused in Chat tab |
| `frontend/src/components/molecules/ViolationCard.tsx` | Moves to Explanation tab only |
| `frontend/src/components/molecules/SuggestionCard.tsx` | Stays in Explanation tab only |
| `frontend/src/lib/ai/prompts.ts` | Add tag-generation prompt block |
| `frontend/src/app/api/theory-inspector/route.ts` | Add `suggestedTags` field to streaming response |

---

## Phase 1 — Theory Inspector Tab Redesign + Iteration 1 Fixes

### 1.1 Two-Tab Layout

**Goal:** Split TheoryInspectorPanel into two mutually exclusive tabs. Default: Explanation.

**State changes** (`useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`):
\```typescript
activeTab: "explanation" | "chat";   // default: "explanation"
setActiveTab: (tab: "explanation" | "chat") => void;
\```

**Tab bar UI** (top of panel body, below header):
- Two pill-style toggle buttons: "Explanation" | "Chat"
- Active tab: filled background using `--color-surface-accent` design token
- Inactive tab: ghost style
- No underline tabs — pill toggle matches HarmonyForge design system

**Explanation tab renders** (in order):
1. This Note card (see §1.2)
2. Show/Hide Rationale toggle + rationale blocks (unchanged logic)
3. Tutor Summary section
4. Ideas to Try Next section (with IdeaAction accept/reject buttons = Quick Edits)
5. Violation cards (RedLine audit results — Explanation tab only per user decision)
6. Suggestion cards (accept/reject corrections)
7. No input field. No chat bubble list.

**Chat tab renders**:
1. Chat message list (system, user, ai bubbles, intent cards, dividers)
2. Starter tags strip at bottom (see §1.4)
3. Input field + Send button

**Files to modify:**
- `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx`: add tab bar JSX, conditionally render tab content blocks, remove input bar from Explanation render path
- `useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`: add `activeTab` + `setActiveTab`

---

### 1.2 "This Note" Visual Distinction

**Goal:** This Note card must be visually distinct from Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next.

**Treatment:** Left accent border (`border-l-4 border-primary`) + darker card background (`bg-surface-elevated` or `bg-zinc-800/60` if token unavailable) + `rounded-r-md` on the card.

**Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next:** flat sections, `bg-transparent`, section label only (`text-xs font-semibold text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide`), no card border.

**Exact JSX structure in `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx`**:
\```tsx
{/* This Note */}
<div className="border-l-4 border-primary bg-zinc-800/60 rounded-r-md px-3 py-2 mb-3">
  <span className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-primary">This Note</span>
  {/* pitch, voice, slot, original vs current */}
</div>

{/* Tutor Summary */}
<div className="mb-3">
  <span className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground">Tutor Summary</span>
  {/* aiExplanation */}
</div>

{/* Ideas to Try Next */}
<div className="mb-3">
  <span className="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground">Ideas to Try Next</span>
  {/* aiSuggestions + ideaAction buttons */}
</div>
\```

---

### 1.3 Quick Edits — Explanation Tab Only

**Constraint:** IdeaAction accept/reject buttons (the "Quick Edits") render only in the Explanation tab's "Ideas to Try Next" section. They do not appear anywhere in the Chat tab.

No logic change needed — the IdeaAction buttons already live inside the note insight card render path. Confirming they remain there and are not duplicated in chat bubble rendering.

---

### 1.4 Starter Tags (Chat Tab) — AI-Generated, Removable

**Goal:** Chat tab shows a strip of contextual tag chips at the bottom (above input field). As conversation progresses, AI suggests new tags. User can remove any tag.

**State additions** (`useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`):
\```typescript
chatTags: string[];                        // active tag list
setChatTags: (tags: string[]) => void;
removeTag: (tag: string) => void;          // removes permanently for session
addTags: (newTags: string[]) => void;      // deduplicates before adding
\```

**Initial starter tags** (hardcoded seed, always present on first open):
\```typescript
const SEED_TAGS = [
  "Why this note?",
  "Check my voice leading",
  "Suggest a passing chord",
  "What else fits here?",
  "Show voice motion",
];
\```

**Tag generation after each AI response** (`useTheoryInspector.ts`):
- After `sendMessage()` completes streaming, parse `<<<TAGS>>>` block from response (similar to `<<<INTENT>>>`)
- Format: `<<<TAGS>>>\n["tag1","tag2","tag3"]\n<<<END_TAGS>>>`
- Call `store.addTags(parsedTags)` — deduplicates, max 8 total tags at any time (oldest non-seed tags drop first if limit exceeded)

**Prompt change** (`prompts.ts` → `buildSystemPrompt()`):
- Add to system prompt: after each reply, output `<<<TAGS>>>` JSON array of 2–3 contextual follow-up question tags that are specific to the current score context.

**API route change** (`/api/theory-inspector/route.ts`):
- No structural change needed — tags piggyback on the existing streaming text stream, same as INTENT blocks.

**Tag strip UI** (Chat tab, above input bar):
\```
[Why this note?  ×]  [Show voice motion  ×]  [Try tritone sub  ×]
\```
- Each tag: `rounded-full bg-surface-elevated border border-border px-2.5 py-1 text-xs flex items-center gap-1`
- `×` button on each tag: calls `removeTag(tag)` — no confirmation needed
- Clicking tag body: calls `onChipClick(tag)` → prefills and sends as user message
- Strip is horizontally scrollable if overflow
- Strip collapses to 0px height if `chatTags.length === 0`

**Files to modify:**
- `useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`: add `chatTags`, `setChatTags`, `removeTag`, `addTags`
- `useTheoryInspector.ts`: parse `<<<TAGS>>>` block post-stream, call `addTags`
- `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx`: render tag strip in Chat tab above input bar
- `prompts.ts`: add TAGS generation instruction to system prompt
- `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` props: add `onTagRemove?: (tag: string) => void`, `chatTags?: string[]`

---

### 1.5 Iteration 1 Remaining Fixes

**Theory Inspector LLM verbosity** (`prompts.ts`):
- Enforce existing brevity rule more strictly: max 3–5 bullets per reply in tutor persona. Add hard rule: "Never write prose paragraphs. Every point is a bullet."

**Toolbar button labels** (investigate `frontend/src/components/organisms/ScoreToolbar.tsx` or equivalent):
- Add `aria-label` and visible text label beneath each icon button. Use `flex-col items-center gap-0.5` with `text-[10px]` label.

**Cursor/hover affordances for note elements** (VexFlow canvas interaction layer):
- Ensure `cursor-pointer` and `cursor-grab`/`cursor-grabbing` CSS classes are applied on note SVG elements during hover/drag states. Locate note interaction handlers in `frontend/src/components/organisms/ScoreEditor.tsx` or VexFlow wrapper.

---

## Phase 2 — Stability, Persistence & Iteration 2 Fixes

### 2.1 State Persistence Across Navigation

**Problem:** User parameters (key, mode, settings) drop when navigating between document and sandbox pages.

**Fix:** Move `key`, `mode`, `rhythmDensity`, `pickupBeats` from local component state into a persistent Zustand store (likely `useDocumentStore.ts` or `useUploadStore.ts`). Confirm these stores use `persist` middleware. If not, add `persist` with `localStorage` as storage.

**Files:** Locate `useUploadStore.ts` or `useDocumentStore.ts`; confirm or add `persist` middleware from `zustand/middleware`.

### 2.2 Anacrusis/Pickup Measure Support

**Problem:** System treats pickup measures as full measures, causing misaligned harmonic rhythm.

**UI fix:** Add a "Pickup beats" numeric input (0–3) to the document configuration page. Wire to `set_pickup_beats` intent (already exists in `intentRouter.ts`). This value passes to the backend generation endpoint.

**Files:** `frontend/src/app/document/page.tsx` — add pickup beats field to the pre-generation settings form.

### 2.3 Rest Placeholders on Note Deletion

**Problem:** Deleting a note leaves visual gaps instead of rests.

**Fix:** In the note deletion handler (locate in `ScoreEditor.tsx` or note mutation utils), after removing a note, insert a rest of equal duration at the same position. This is a frontend symbolic edit — produces a rest note object in the `EditableScore` JSON.

### 2.4 Plain-Language Terminology

**Replace these terms universally:**
| Current | Replace with |
|---|---|
| "SATB" | "4-part harmony" (with tooltip: "Soprano, Alto, Tenor, Bass") |
| "backing chords" | "accompaniment chords" |
| "portal / mixed / flowing" | "chordal (slow) / mixed / flowing (active)" with brief musical description |
| "harmonic rhythm" | "chord change speed" (in UI labels; preserve in Theory Inspector explanations) |

Apply via global find-replace in all UI label strings, NOT in AI prompts or music theory explanations.

### 2.5 Rhythmic Complexity Control

**Add:** Radio/toggle in document page pre-generation settings: "Rhythmic style: Chordal (whole/half notes) | Mixed | Flowing (quarter/eighth notes)". Maps to existing `set_rhythm_density` intent value.

### 2.6 Theory Inspector Responsive Design

**Fix:** Ensure the panel's scroll container (`overflow-y-auto`) applies only to the tab body, not the entire panel. Header and tab bar must be sticky (`sticky top-0`). Input bar must be sticky bottom. Confirm `max-h` is set relative to viewport, not fixed px.

### 2.7 MIDI Playback Stability

**Investigate:** Locate Tone.js playback initialization in `frontend/src/hooks/usePlayback.ts` or equivalent. Common fixes:
- Ensure `Tone.start()` is called inside a user gesture handler, not on mount
- Ensure master volume is set to audible level (e.g., `Tone.Destination.volume.value = -6`)
- Add try/catch around `Transport.start()` to prevent crashes from propagating to UI

---

## Phase 3 — Context-Aware Analysis & Localized Editing (Iteration 3)

### 3.1 Context-Aware Chord Analysis

**Problem:** Theory Inspector analyzes chords in isolation — misses ii-V-I cadences, tritone subs, sequential voice motion.

**Fix** (`prompts.ts` → `buildSystemPrompt()`):
- Add "Progression Window" section to system prompt: when `inspectorScoreFocus` contains a note, include the 3 slots before and after in the FACT block.
- Add prompt instruction: "Evaluate harmonic function sequentially. Identify the cadential pattern (authentic, half, deceptive, plagal). Identify any tritone substitutions or secondary dominants before commenting on individual pitches."
- This requires `buildProgressionWindowFacts()` (already exists in `noteExplainContext.ts`) to be included in every tutor call, not just note-explain calls.

### 3.2 Localized Highlight-to-Generate

**Scope constraint:** Classical-only baseline. No jazz or pop genre scope for this feature.

**UI:** Add "Regenerate this bar" button on measure focus card (already has `onEditFocusedRegion` prop). When clicked, sends a localized regeneration request to backend with measure index + current notes as constraints. Backend scope: separate endpoint or flag on existing generation endpoint — frontend only wires the UI and passes `{ measureIndex, lockedPitches }`.

**Frontend changes only:**
- Render "Regenerate this bar" button in measure focus card view in Explanation tab
- Pass `measureIndex` and current note pitches as payload to `onEditFocusedRegion()` callback
- Backend implementation out of scope for frontend phase

### 3.3 Expressive Sovereignty Boundary

**Rule:** The frontend must never insert dynamic markings, articulations, phrasing slurs, staccatos, or legatos programmatically. Confirm no such insertions exist in score mutation utilities. Add a lint-comment guard where relevant.

---

## Phase 4 — Discoverability & Navigation (Iteration 4)

### 4.1 Navigational Discoverability

**Fix:** Rename the document tab/link to "Edit Configuration" or "Settings". Update all navigation labels referencing the document page. Add a visible "← Back to Editor" button in the sandbox header when arriving from document page.

**Files:** `frontend/src/app/sandbox/page.tsx` (header nav), `frontend/src/app/document/page.tsx` (back link).

### 4.2 Interactive Affordances Onboarding

**Fix:** On first sandbox load (tracked via `localStorage` flag `hf_onboarding_seen`), show a 3-step tooltip overlay:
1. "Click any note to get an AI explanation" (points to first note)
2. "Drag notes to move them" (points to score canvas)
3. "Open Theory Inspector to chat with your AI tutor" (points to inspector toggle)

Overlay dismisses on "Got it" click. Never shown again after dismissal.

**Files:** Create `frontend/src/components/molecules/OnboardingOverlay.tsx`. Mount in `sandbox/page.tsx` conditionally.

### 4.3 Tactile Feedback

- Every note click must produce an immediate visual state change (selected ring, color shift) within one render frame — no perceptible lag.
- If a click fails to register (interaction glitch), show a subtle toast: "Tap a note to select it."

---

## Phase 5 — Accessibility & Inclusion (Iteration 5)

### 5.1 Plain-Language Theory Translations

**Explanation level already exists** (`explanationLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "professional"`). Enforce in UI:
- Default explanation level to `"beginner"` for new sessions (currently unknown default — confirm in store init).
- Display the level selector prominently in the Theory Inspector header (B / I / P pill toggle, already present per architecture report).
- In `prompts.ts`: strengthen beginner-level prompt rules — "Use no academic terminology without immediately defining it in parentheses."

### 5.2 Progressive Disclosure & Reset

**Add:** "Reset workspace" button in sandbox header (next to "Edit Configuration"). Clicking shows a confirmation modal: "This will clear all edits and return to the last generated score." On confirm: reload from `useUploadStore` baseline.

**Add:** Collapsible "Advanced settings" section in document page that hides pickup beats, rhythmic density, and genre behind a disclosure toggle. Default: collapsed.

### 5.3 Instrument Selection Clarification

**Instrument setup page (document page):**
- Group instruments by family: Strings, Woodwinds, Brass, Voices
- Add instrument icon (already have SVG files in `frontend/public/instruments/`)
- Add brief descriptor under each name: e.g., "Violin I — high strings, melody lead"
- Future: auditory preview (out of scope for this phase — mark with `// TODO: audio preview`)

### 5.4 Trackpad & Keyboard Optimization

- Ensure all drag interactions have keyboard equivalents: arrow keys move selected note by semitone (up/down) or beat position (left/right)
- Add `touch-action: none` on score canvas to prevent scroll interference during drag on trackpad
- Implement tap-to-select (single tap) vs tap-to-move (tap selected note then tap destination) as alternative to drag

---

## Verification Plan

### Phase 1
1. Open sandbox → Theory Inspector panel shows two tabs ("Explanation" / "Chat")
2. Default tab is Explanation; no input field visible
3. Click a note → This Note card appears with accent border + dark bg; Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next are flat sections below
4. IdeaAction accept/reject buttons appear only in Explanation tab
5. Switch to Chat tab → input field visible; tag strip visible with seed tags
6. Send a message → AI responds; new tags appear in strip; old tags persist
7. Click × on a tag → tag removed, does not reappear
8. Click a tag → prefills and sends as message

### Phase 2
1. Set key + mode on document page → navigate to sandbox → navigate back → values retained
2. Delete a note → measure shows rest placeholder, not a gap
3. "4-part harmony" appears everywhere "SATB" was used; tooltip on hover shows full term

### Phase 3
1. Click a note → Tutor Summary references the surrounding chord progression context, not just the isolated pitch
2. Click a measure → "Regenerate this bar" button visible in Explanation tab

### Phase 4
1. First sandbox visit: onboarding overlay appears with 3 steps; dismisses on "Got it"
2. Second visit: no overlay
3. Navigation label reads "Edit Configuration" not "document"

### Phase 5
1. New session: explanation level defaults to "beginner"
2. Beginner-level explanations define all terms inline
3. Arrow keys move selected note by semitone
4. "Reset workspace" clears edits and returns to generated score baseline

1	# HarmonyForge: 5-Phase Implementation Plan (Iterations 1–5)
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Five rounds of user studies surfaced a consistent pattern: the system's core music engine is trusted, but the interface creates cognitive overload, discoverability failures, and fragile state. The Theory Inspector—the Glass Box's primary output surface—is simultaneously the most valued and most confusing component. This plan addresses every finding across all five iterations, structured as five sequential phases. Phase 1 includes the Theory Inspector tab redesign as its architectural foundation, since all downstream inspector work depends on the new layout.

26	## Phase 1 — Theory Inspector Tab Redesign + Iteration 1 Fixes
27	
28	### 1.1 Two-Tab Layout
29	
30	**Goal:** Split TheoryInspectorPanel into two mutually exclusive tabs. Default: Explanation.
31	
32	**State changes** (`useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`):
33	```typescript
34	activeTab: "explanation" | "chat";   // default: "explanation"
35	setActiveTab: (tab: "explanation" | "chat") => void;
36	```
37	
38	**Tab bar UI** (top of panel body, below header):
39	- Two pill-style toggle buttons: "Explanation" | "Chat"
40	- Active tab: filled background using `--color-surface-accent` design token
41	- Inactive tab: ghost style
42	- No underline tabs — pill toggle matches HarmonyForge design system
43	
44	**Explanation tab renders** (in order):
45	1. This Note card (see §1.2)
46	2. Show/Hide Rationale toggle + rationale blocks (unchanged logic)
47	3. Tutor Summary section
48	4. Ideas to Try Next section (with IdeaAction accept/reject buttons = Quick Edits)
49	5. Violation cards (RedLine audit results — Explanation tab only per user decision)
50	6. Suggestion cards (accept/reject corrections)
51	7. No input field. No chat bubble list.

55	2. Starter tags strip at bottom (see §1.4)

60	- `useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`: add `activeTab` + `setActiveTab`

64	### 1.2 "This Note" Visual Distinction
65	
66	**Goal:** This Note card must be visually distinct from Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next.
67	
68	**Treatment:** Left accent border (`border-l-4 border-primary`) + darker card background (`bg-surface-elevated` or `bg-zinc-800/60` if token unavailable) + `rounded-r-md` on the card.
69	
70	**Tutor Summary and Ideas to Try Next:** flat sections, `bg-transparent`, section label only (`text-xs font-semibold text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide`), no card border.
71

73	```tsx

78	</div>

83	  {/* aiExplanation */}
84	</div>

95	### 1.3 Quick Edits — Explanation Tab Only

98	
99	No logic change needed — the IdeaAction buttons already live inside the note insight card render path. Confirming they remain there and are not duplicated in chat bubble rendering.

103	### 1.4 Starter Tags (Chat Tab) — AI-Generated, Removable
104	
105	**Goal:** Chat tab shows a strip of contextual tag chips at the bottom (above input field). As conversation progresses, AI suggests new tags. User can remove any tag.
106	
107	**State additions** (`useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`):
108	```typescript
109	chatTags: string[];                        // active tag list
110	setChatTags: (tags: string[]) => void;
111	removeTag: (tag: string) => void;          // removes permanently for session
112	addTags: (newTags: string[]) => void;      // deduplicates before adding
113	```
114	
115	**Initial starter tags** (hardcoded seed, always present on first open):
116	```typescript
117	const SEED_TAGS = [
118	  "Why this note?",
119	  "Check my voice leading",
120	  "Suggest a passing chord",
121	  "What else fits here?",
122	  "Show voice motion",
123	];
124	```
125	
126	**Tag generation after each AI response** (`useTheoryInspector.ts`):
127	- After `sendMessage()` completes streaming, parse `<<<TAGS>>>` block from response (similar to `<<<INTENT>>>`)
128	- Format: `<<<TAGS>>>\n["tag1","tag2","tag3"]\n<<<END_TAGS>>>`
129	- Call `store.addTags(parsedTags)` — deduplicates, max 8 total tags at any time (oldest non-seed tags drop first if limit exceeded)
130	
131	**Prompt change** (`prompts.ts` → `buildSystemPrompt()`):
132	- Add to system prompt: after each reply, output `<<<TAGS>>>` JSON array of 2–3 contextual follow-up question tags that are specific to the current score context.
133	
134	**API route change** (`/api/theory-inspector/route.ts`):
135	- No structural change needed — tags piggyback on the existing streaming text stream, same as INTENT blocks.
136	
137	**Tag strip UI** (Chat tab, above input bar):
138	```
139	[Why this note?  ×]  [Show voice motion  ×]  [Try tritone sub  ×]
140	```
141	- Each tag: `rounded-full bg-surface-elevated border border-border px-2.5 py-1 text-xs flex items-center gap-1`
142	- `×` button on each tag: calls `removeTag(tag)` — no confirmation needed
143	- Clicking tag body: calls `onChipClick(tag)` → prefills and sends as user message
144	- Strip is horizontally scrollable if overflow
145	- Strip collapses to 0px height if `chatTags.length === 0`
146	
147	**Files to modify:**
148	- `useTheoryInspectorStore.ts`: add `chatTags`, `setChatTags`, `removeTag`, `addTags`
149	- `useTheoryInspector.ts`: parse `<<<TAGS>>>` block post-stream, call `addTags`
150	- `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx`: render tag strip in Chat tab above input bar
151	- `prompts.ts`: add TAGS generation instruction to system prompt
152	- `TheoryInspectorPanel.tsx` props: add `onTagRemove?: (tag: string) => void`, `chatTags?: string[]`
153	
154	---
155	
156	### 1.5 Iteration 1 Remaining Fixes
157	
158	**Theory Inspector LLM verbosity** (`prompts.ts`):
159	- Enforce existing brevity rule more strictly: max 3–5 bullets per reply in tutor persona. Add hard rule: "Never write prose paragraphs. Every point is a bullet."
160	
161	**Toolbar button labels** (investigate `frontend/src/components/organisms/ScoreToolbar.tsx` or equivalent):
162	- Add `aria-label` and visible text label beneath each icon button. Use `flex-col items-center gap-0.5` with `text-[10px]` label.
163	
164	**Cursor/hover affordances for note elements** (VexFlow canvas interaction layer):
165	- Ensure `cursor-pointer` and `cursor-grab`/`cursor-grabbing` CSS classes are applied on note SVG elements during hover/drag states. Locate note interaction handlers in `frontend/src/components/organisms/ScoreEditor.tsx` or VexFlow wrapper.
166
